purpose: Audit the copied vbtprobuild Wave 1-11 workflow reports against this repo's current starter template and identify where this repo is weak as a durable build-governance program.

wave_1_11_baseline.evidence_rule: Focused validators and focused tests prove ordinary owner behavior; generated indexes, receipts, checkpoints, and reports are discovery or closeout evidence and need explicit ownership.
wave_1_11_baseline.refresh_rule: Refresh broad indexes only for explicit discovery/reconciliation work, stale discovery blockers, real next-wave dependency, closeout gates, or operator request.
wave_1_11_baseline.receipt_rule: Do not write per-wave receipts by default; reserve receipts/checkpoints for writers, generators, materializers, external actions, irreversible decisions, and real closeout gates.

target_repo_strengths.summary: The canonical doctrine now captures the Wave 1-11 cadence: owner bundle, focused proof, implementation commit, conditional generated refresh, and no head chasing.
The starter slice packet validator enforces owner files, required source reads, owning validator, focused tests, boundary rules, structured refresh decision, and commit plan.

The repo has useful first-tier starter tools: low-token contract validation, source-read register validation, planned-future-surface validation, repo file inventory, command map, read-only command harness, release validation, and bootstrap smoke tests.

weaknesses.title: The template promises a mature control-plane surface set that it does not implement.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 succeeded because helpers, validators, indexes, read-only commands, worktree noise, and evidence surfaces became explicit owner surfaces when needed. The repo-assist audits show stale or incomplete helper/script catalogs create routing misses and duplicated or unsafe behavior.
target_repo_evidence: pyproject.toml declares minimum helpers including query_authority, repo map validation, config/schema/contract map, helper catalog, validator map, artifact output map, worktree cleanliness, no-secret persistence, protected-surface guard, output post-hooks, refresh indexes, and repo health gate.

The current scripts directory only implements the starter subset: slice packet, low-token, source-read, planned-future-surfaces, repo file index, command map, read-only harness, mature migration, release, bootstrap, and rendering.
weaknesses.risk: A new repo can read the methodology and believe it has more machine enforcement than it actually has. Agents may treat prose-only guarantees as validated guarantees.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Add an authority-map or implemented-surface matrix that distinguishes implemented starter controls from planned controls, and make release/bootstrap checks fail if promised controls are listed as active but no script/schema/test exists.

weaknesses.title: No plan-note or authority index exists, even though the workflow depends on distinguishing active authority from evidence-only notes.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: The Wave 1-11 evidence chain says the active crack-repair plan was the running ledger, plan-note outputs moved when plan/future details changed, and active/historical/evidence-only status prevented superseded notes from controlling next action.
target_repo_evidence: repo_agnostic_durable_slice_build_workflow_methodology_20260514.json describes a plan_note_and_slice_packet_index and plan_note_index responsibilities.
There is no scripts/build_plan_note_index.py, scripts/query_plan_note_index.py, schemas/plan_note_index.schema.json, manifests/plan_note_index.json, or root active-set artifact.
bootstrap_local_repo.py creates plans/repo_roadmap.json and plans/slices/slice_001_packet.json, but creates no plan-note index or active-set file.
weaknesses.risk: As soon as more reports, packets, and future notes exist, future agents must infer current authority manually. That is exactly the failure mode Wave 1-11 repaired.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Implement a small JSON plan-note index over plans, packets, reports, and source-read refs with fields for path, status, authority_class, selected_next_action, supersession, and validation refs.

weaknesses.title: Evidence artifacts are not first-class indexed outputs; imported evidence is currently classified as source by the file inventory.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 treats reports, receipts, checkpoints, and generated outputs as evidence that must be distinguishable from source authority and generated noise.
target_repo_evidence: repo_agnostic_durable_slice_build_workflow_methodology_20260514.json describes an artifact_evidence_index with artifact kind, writer/generator, source commit/run id, checksums, secret-safe status, receipt/checkpoint refs, and validation refs.
plans/planned_future_surfaces.json marks artifact_output_map as planned_not_created.
scripts/build_repo_file_index.py classifies only manifests, reports, and receipts as generated_or_evidence; the new evidence/ directory and checkpoints are classified as source.
weaknesses.risk: The copied Wave 1-11 reports are durable, but this repo cannot query them by evidence role, authority boundary, source hash, receipt relation, or secret-safe status. Future agents can also mistake imported evidence JSON for source-truth configuration.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Implement artifact_output_map for reports, evidence, receipts, checkpoints, manifests, and generated outputs; update repo_file_index classification so evidence/ and checkpoints/ are evidence, not source.

weaknesses.title: Worktree noise classification is doctrine-only.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 closeout depended on classifying stale index, exact-path, scanner, handoff, and superseded-plan findings as evidence unless paired with a required validator/test/safety failure.
target_repo_evidence: repo_agnostic_durable_slice_build_workflow_methodology_20260514.json names worktree_cleanliness_classifier as a required script architecture role.
pyproject.toml declares validate_worktree_cleanliness, but no scripts/validate_worktree_cleanliness.py or policy/schema/test exists.

The read-only harness compares before/after snapshots for configured commands, but it does not classify the current dirty worktree into generated drift, raw evidence, user changes, protected mutations, or action-required dirt.
weaknesses.risk: Agents can still overreact to untracked evidence, stale generated indexes, or user edits, or underreact to protected mutations. The no-head-chasing rule remains largely procedural.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Add a read-only worktree cleanliness classifier with a JSON policy for generated evidence, raw scratch, protected paths, user changes, and action-required dirt.

weaknesses.title: Secret and private-material checks do not scan persisted report/evidence content.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: The Wave 1-11 closeout and index audits explicitly checked that secrets and tokenized private URLs were not persisted, and they kept private docs endpoints symbolic.
target_repo_evidence: contracts/read_only_command_harness.json enables stdout/stderr secret scanning for commands.
scripts/validate_read_only_commands.py scans command stdout/stderr, not tracked repository file content.
scripts/validate_release_package.py blocks secret-like path names but does not scan file contents for tokenized URLs or secret assignments.

The copied evidence currently uses symbolic private endpoint refs and no assignment-like credential values were found by a narrow scan, but the repo has no validator that enforces this for future reports.
weaknesses.risk: A future imported report, receipt, or generated index could persist a credential-like value while release checks still pass if the path name is benign.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Add validate_no_secrets_persisted.py covering reports, evidence, receipts, checkpoints, manifests, docs, and generated outputs, with allowlisted symbolic refs.

weaknesses.title: Receipt and checkpoint semantics are documented but not represented by schema, validator, or starter writer.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 avoided status-confetti receipts by writing receipts/checkpoints only for generated index runs and post-Wave-11 closeout proof.
target_repo_evidence: AGENTS.md and SKILL.md state the receipt/checkpoint rule.
There is no schemas/receipt.schema.json, schemas/checkpoint.schema.json, scripts/validate_receipts.py, scripts/write_closeout_receipt.py, or reports/receipts policy binding.
plans/planned_future_surfaces.json does not include a receipt/checkpoint schema or validator activation slice.
weaknesses.risk: Different users or agents can interpret receipts inconsistently: either writing status spam or skipping meaningful closeout evidence when a writer/generator/materializer actually needs proof.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Define minimal receipt/checkpoint schemas and a validator that rejects status-only receipts while accepting writer/generator/materializer/closeout receipts.

weaknesses.title: The repo has conditional refresh doctrine but no generated-refresh orchestrator or post-output hook surface.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 refreshed repo/script/config/output indexes only when a packet or closeout gate required it, then committed generated outputs once and stopped.
target_repo_evidence: The slice packet validator checks structured refresh_decision fields.
pyproject.toml declares run_output_post_hooks and refresh_indexes roles, but no corresponding scripts, schemas, contracts, or tests exist.
Makefile has repo-index and repo-index-check, but no plan-note index, artifact map, config inventory, output schema, or post-output hook commands.
weaknesses.risk: A packet can say refresh is required, but the template has no common runner that maps refresh_decision flags to exact safe commands, validation, and generated-refresh commit boundaries.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Add a small refresh decision executor or command map extension that names which generated surfaces exist locally, which flags they satisfy, and which validation commands prove them.

weaknesses.title: Release validation can pass while newly imported evidence remains untracked or unclassified.
weaknesses.workflow_report_basis: Wave 1-11 used committed evidence and targeted index extracts so future agents could find the proof chain without chat memory.
target_repo_evidence: The current audit import leaves evidence/ untracked until explicitly committed.
scripts/validate_release_package.py reads git ls-files for tracked/package checks, so untracked evidence is invisible to release validation.
make check passed after evidence import because no release rule requires new evidence reports to be tracked, indexed, or source-read registered beyond the manual source_read_register update.
weaknesses.risk: Important imported workflow evidence can sit outside git or outside generated indexes even while the repo reports healthy.
weaknesses.recommended_next_slice: Extend release or health validation to warn when source_read_register evidence refs point at untracked files, and require promoted evidence reports to appear in artifact_output_map once that map exists.

recommended_slice_order.goal: Separate active starter controls from planned controls so users do not overtrust prose-only guarantees.
Make active roadmap, packets, evidence-only reports, and superseded notes queryable.
Index reports, evidence, receipts, checkpoints, manifests, hashes, source refs, and secret-safe status.
Classify generated drift, raw evidence, protected mutations, user changes, and action-required dirt.
Scan repo artifacts, reports, receipts, checkpoints, and docs for secret-like or tokenized private material.
Make meaningful receipts/checkpoints enforceable without status-confetti artifacts.
Map refresh_decision flags to concrete local generated-surface commands and validation.

bottom_line: This repo is strong as a portable doctrine and starter bootstrap, but weak as a self-auditing build-governance control plane. The highest leverage fix is to stop letting pyproject and methodology list planned controls as if they were active, then add the missing authority/evidence/worktree/secret indexes one small validator-led slice at a time.
